claim: when several drop conditions hold for one transaction, report only the single highest-priority reason (1..14); reasons 1..8 are packet drops, 9..14 assembly drops
lesson: The ANY mutant only manifests when cond == only-bit-13 (timeout alone), ~1/16384 per cycle, so 2000-cycle random sim MISSED it (verilator=PASS) while formal caught it (verilator=PASS but formal=FAIL). Live evidence that formal closes coverage gaps random sim leaves open.
caveats: Combinational priority encoder; the 14 reason CONDITIONS themselves are produced by the upstream decode/assembly logic (this module only proves the priority+class policy).
Reflects the policy of v3 §10.3; the per-reason condition derivations (PD_MALFORMED_TLP etc.) are separate decode contracts.
